---
title: "Heart - Learning Objectives"
format: html
---

::: {.callout-tip icon="false"}

## Learning objectives

After completing this chapter, learners will be able to:

- Explain the indications and limitations of point-of-care ultrasound cardiac examination.
- Obtain all standard transthoracic views
- Recognize normal anatomy in each view.
- Identify:
  - mild and severe alteration left ventricle ejection fraction using visual semi-quantitative assessment, EPSS and MAPSE
  - pericardial effusion and consequences on right ventricle
  - increased intravascular volume using vena cava diameter and respiratory variation
  - signs of acute cor pulmonare
- Interpret findings according to the clinical context.
- Recognize common pitfalls and avoid frequent mistakes.

Note that the use of doppler imaging (including colour, pulsed, continuous and tissue) is out of the scope of this chapter.

:::
